---
title: "SynaptogenEM: Overview"
author:
  - name: Fatima Al-Rashidi
    affiliations:
      - Corane Farm Research
  - name: Lucas Moreira
    affiliations:
      - Corane Farm Research
date: 2026-02-01
---

## Introduction

SynaptogenEM applies correlative light and electron microscopy (CLEM) to study the ultrastructural basis of activity-dependent synaptogenesis in developing sensorimotor circuits. By combining optogenetic stimulation with serial FIB-SEM, we capture synapse formation and stabilization events at nanometer resolution and link molecular identity — reported by fluorescent markers — to structural outcome.

## Goals

1. Map the timeline of synapse formation in sensorimotor circuits from P5 to P21 at ultrastructural resolution
2. Determine how patterned optogenetic activity shifts the balance between synapse formation and elimination
3. Identify morphological signatures that predict synapse survival vs. retraction

## Current status

::: {.callout-tip}
## Status: Active development
SynaptogenEM is under active development. CLEM acquisition at P10 is complete; automated synapse classification is in progress.
:::

Correlative imaging at P5 and P10 time points is complete. We have acquired ~200 µm³ volumes per animal (n = 4 per condition) and are currently training the synapse maturation classifier on manually annotated ground truth. Optogenetic stimulation protocols have been validated in acute slice.

## Related resources

- [Link to code repository](https://github.com/)
- [Link to data](https://example.com/)
- [Related publication](/publications.qmd)

## Version

Document version: {{< meta git-version >}}
